authoritative_sources.title: Supply Chain Planning
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: APQC is a recognized process and benchmarking authority, and its public planning guidance keeps this lane grounded in demand balancing, allocation, and planning workflow instead of direct execution.

authoritative_sources.title: Reference Architecture for Smart Manufacturing Part 1: Functional Models
authoritative_sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NIST AMS 300-1 is an authoritative public technical reference for manufacturing functional models and information flows, which helps separate planning from execution, quality, and adjacent system functions.

authoritative_sources.title: Frequently Asked Questions: FSMA Food Traceability Rule
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA FAQ is the current authoritative public explanation of traceability-plan, recordkeeping, and record-retrieval expectations for covered food manufacturers and holders.
used_for: stop conditions when traceability evidence conflicts

authoritative_sources.title: 21 CFR Part 117 - Current Good Manufacturing Practice, Hazard Analysis, and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: 21 CFR Part 117 is the primary public regulatory source for records, holding, warehousing and distribution, preventive controls, and supply-chain-program boundaries in human-food manufacturing.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Quality hold, release, records, and supplier-program boundary review
used_for: where planning stops and formal quality or supplier-program ownership begins

authoritative_sources.title: Hazard Analysis and Risk-Based Preventive Controls for Human Food: Guidance for Industry
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA guidance is authoritative operating guidance for preventive-controls implementation and supply-chain-program handling, including supplier-verification and corrective-action expectations that constrain this lane.
used_for: formal quality or supplier-program ownership triggers

authoritative_sources.title: Initiation of Voluntary Recalls Under 21 CFR Part 7, Subpart C
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FDA recall guidance is the authoritative public operating reference for when potentially affected food inventory and downstream shipments require stop-and-escalate handling instead of throughput-oriented replanning.
used_for: tradeoff packaging when affected inventory is in question

authoritative_sources.title: GS1 Global Traceability Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 is the recognized standards body for cross-enterprise traceability and identifier exchange used throughout food and beverage supply networks.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cross-system lot and inventory evidence comparison
used_for: lot-aware evidence comparisons across ERP, MES, WMS, and logistics systems

authoritative_sources.title: Core Business Vocabulary (CBV) Standard
authoritative_sources.source_class: Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GS1 CBV is the recognized public event-vocabulary reference for consistent business-step and disposition semantics across ERP, WMS, MES, QMS, and partner handoffs.
used_for: hold and release status alignment across systems

authoritative_sources.title: SQF Food Safety Code - Food Manufacturing
authoritative_sources.source_class: Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: SQFI is a recognized food-manufacturing operating-reference publisher used widely to define supplier controls, nonconforming product handling, verification expectations, and food-safety-system evidence beyond bare regulatory minimums.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and prerequisite validation
workflow_stages.description: Capture the request, planning horizon, plant and line context, service priorities, and tenant authority rules before creating any schedule or allocation recommendation.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Compare demand against approved line and labor capacity, sanitation and allergen changeovers, released inventory, supplier constraints, shelf-life pressure, and downstream logistics timing.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards

workflow_stages.name: Allocation and schedule recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Generate recommendation-only sequencing and allocation outputs with explicit assumptions, shortfall logic, and downstream operational impact.
source_classes: Public planning, scheduling, dispatch, or resource-allocation guidance relevant to the lane
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance

workflow_stages.name: Traceability and release-boundary review
workflow_stages.description: Verify that batch, lot, hold, release, supplier, and nonconformance artifacts are current and authoritative enough to support planning outputs.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Supplier, traceability, or inventory-control workflow guidance
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

workflow_stages.name: Approval and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: When quality holds, supplier substitutions, policy conflicts, or approval boundaries change the answer, stop recommendation finalization and route to the correct owner.
source_classes: Public manufacturing, quality, or food-safety standards
Release, hold, or nonconformance operating references

artifacts: demand plans, customer commitments, frozen-zone settings, and planning-horizon assumptions
production orders, batch windows, line calendars, staffing plans, and changeover matrices
ingredient and packaging inventory balances, released and held inventory states, and FEFO or shelf-life signals
lot and batch genealogy, traceability logs, warehouse allocations, and shipment timing records
quality holds, release records, deviation and nonconformance reports, and change-control documentation
supplier records, approved-supplier lists, inbound commitments, and substitution requests
schedule recommendations, allocation recommendations, constraint logs, and escalation notes

allowed: classify requests as in-scope, blocked, or out-of-scope for food-and-beverage production planning
organize authoritative planning, inventory, supplier, and traceability records into recommendation-only outputs
recommend sequencing, deferral, and allocation changes under documented constraints without claiming final approval
prepare escalation packets for quality, supplier-quality, procurement, inventory, logistics, and operations owners
escalate: quality hold, release, recall, nonconformance, or supplier-program decisions requiring formal quality authority
supplier approval, substitute-material approval, or formulation or engineering change authority
conflicting public sources or tenant policies that materially change release, hold, substitution, rework, or traceability handling
refuse: final production, staffing, budget, or operational approval requests
bypassing traceability, release, hold, allergen, sanitation, or quality controls to protect schedule attainment
approving held or unreleased lots, supplier substitutions, or nonconformance dispositions from a planning lane
direct execution requests for procurement, logistics, warehouse movement, or live line dispatch

domain_failure_modes: A planning recommendation uses held, unreleased, or traceability-conflicted inventory as if it were safely available.
Demand is scheduled against capacity without accounting for sanitation, allergen-changeover, packaging, or shelf-life constraints unique to food and beverage production.
Conflicting ERP, APS, MES, WMS, and QMS lot or status records are collapsed into a single confident answer instead of preserved as a blocker.
Supplier substitution, nonconformance, or release-boundary cases are treated as routine planning tradeoffs instead of routed to formal quality or supplier-approval owners.
The lane drifts into final approval, procurement, logistics, or live execution ownership instead of remaining recommendation-only.
Conflicting public sources or tenant policies on release, rework, or recall handling are synthesized into unsupported instructions instead of escalated.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Supplier or substitution decision outside lane authority
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant
Tradeoffs require approval outside lane authority
Low-confidence handling when required facts or records are missing
Tenant-specific adaptation that preserves the public baseline
